body.u: After failing to get through to the seeboardseaboard, I have still got 4 entrantentrance exams left Bowaters wichwhich I have fialedfailed the steelworks wichwhich I didn’t go to as I didn’t like the people who worked there. Medway dry docks wich I passed as an electrical fitter. I had also got a job at Rochester dock yard but only because me mum and dad knew the training officer. ButtBut I chose Medway dry docks and wrotwrote to the others to say that I won’t be able to join theretheir firm as I have already got a job much closer to home and rather than deprive someone of my place I am writing to tell you that someone may fill my vacant place. I’ll always remember the last day at school no work no nothing infactin fact I think that I whentwent home at lunchtime.

I started work on 1st sept 1978 and brought home the grand total of £20 a week. It may not seem much to you but it got me by. When I was fully trained I was bringing home £100 a week I mean {I?} now you are bringing home £1000 a week 10 times more than I was but things cost 20 times as much.

Anyway on April 22nd 1979 I decided to take my driving lessons and that to most of my £20. But when I passed my driving test it all seemed a waste of time until ApprillApril 1981 when I got my first car a {?} a little sports wichwhich was {later?} to be a waste of time as I had a crash and spent several weeks in hoppitalhospital with a broken {?} and a damaged Retina wichwhich meant I couldn’t see out of 1 eye and that meant I had lost my £100 a week job. After this I got a job at the Royal Doulton potteries, pushing baroughborough loads of clay about this job only brought in £70 a week wichwhich is a difference of £30 a week wichwhichImI'm used tooto. So I looked around for another job and found one with John Laing’s over at the Isle of grain this job paid much better than the other any of the jobs before.
